preamble: The nasalization of the voiceless uvular stop /q/ to the uvular nasal [ɴ] is an example of allophony often triggered by following nasal consonants. This assimilation of manner allows for a smoother transition between consonants in a cluster.

examples.note: Nasal allophone occurring before nasal-initial suffixes.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
